summary: Decompiled a large portion of the Kraid stage ground module, raising grkraid.c toward 97% and adding typed support for Kraid map rotation, hand-triggered camera/events, Kraid wait/position state, and per-stage ground variables. The PR replaced several placeholder functions in src/melee/gr/grkraid.c with C implementations and updated grkraid.h/types.h so the config table and Ground::gv unions had enough structure to express the behavior. The only recorded feedback was the decomp.dev report, which showed one new .data match and major improvements for grKraid_801FEA00, grKraid_801FE440, grKraid_801FEE54, grKraid_801FE818, and grKraid_801FE6D8.
what_changed: Added implementations for previously placeholder Kraid-stage functions: grKraid_801FE440, grKraid_801FE6D8, grKraid_801FE818, grKraid_801FEA00, and grKraid_801FEE54.
Added required includes for camera, grlib, lb_00B0, lb_00F9, and baselib/random to support camera shake/callback calls, JObj position lookup, stack padding, and HSD_Randi usage.
Introduced static inline rand_inline(int a, int b) to reproduce bounded random range behavior used by Kraid map timing.
Added const Vec grKr_803B8278 = {0,0,0}; for the zero vector passed to Camera_80030E44 in map-rotation handling.

Expanded grKr_804D6A08_t from an opaque void* field into named configuration fields: map_time_min, map_time_max, map_time_acl, map_rot_spd_min, map_rot_spd_max, kraid_wait_time, kraid_wait_time_add, and kraid_pos_x[6].
Expanded struct grKraid_GroundVars with x10 and struct grKraid_GroundVars2 with byte-sized state fields, a float selector x8, timer xC, and hand JObj pointers x10/x14.

Changed function prototypes in grkraid.h from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS placeholders to typed signatures for grKraid_801FE6D8(HSD_JObj*, float), grKraid_801FEE54(Ground_GObj*), and grKraid_801FF150(Ground_GObj*).
Changed grKraid_801FF150 from a no-argument stub to an empty function accepting Ground_GObj*, matching its call site in grKraid_801FE440.
smart_moves: Recovered the stage config table before leaning on the code: naming map_time_min/max, map_time_acl, rotation speed bounds, wait times, and kraid_pos_x made the stage logic readable and likely helped the .data section reach 100%.
Modeled Kraid behavior as explicit GroundVars and GroundVars2 fields instead of leaving a byte blob, enabling direct expression of state machines, timers, selected positions, and cached hand JObjs.
Kept matching-sensitive artifacts such as PAD_STACK calls in grKraid_801FEA00 and grKraid_801FEE54, which is a common tactic when the source-level logic is understood but stack layout still matters.
Used a local inline RNG wrapper to match range-order behavior for both ascending and descending bounds rather than simplifying to a single min/max formula that might alter generated code.

Preserved hardware/runtime side effects in the stage behavior: HSD_JObj flag changes, HSD_JObjSetTranslateX, Camera_80030E44, Ground_801C53EC/5440/5544 event calls, OSReport, and animation completion checks.
Cached hand JObjs during initialization using Ground_801C3FA4(gobj, 31) and Ground_801C3FA4(gobj, 47), then reused them in the attack/rotation state machine.
decomp_lessons: For stage files, matching often depends on recovering both behavior code and the per-stage parameter struct; here the header update for grKr_804D6A08_t was tied directly to .data matching and readable code.
Ground variable unions should be incrementally converted from raw padding to typed fields as soon as the state machine reveals byte, timer, float, and pointer roles.

When a stage object coordinates with other ground GObjs, keep Ground_801C2BA4 index usage visible and evidence-grounded; this PR shows Kraid code accessing map object index 3 and companion object index 1.

Do not prematurely rename unknown gv fields beyond the evidence. This PR still uses x0/x1/x2-style names for recovered layout fields while giving clearer names to config-table fields whose purpose was more obvious.

Animation state machines in gr modules commonly combine grAnime_801C7FF8 setup, grAnime_801C83D0 completion checks, frame counters, sound/effect IDs through Ground_801C53EC, and JObj visibility flags.
Signed small fields matter: struct grKraid_GroundVars2 uses s8/u8 fields at exact offsets, so byte signedness and offset preservation are part of matching, not just style.
Even empty stubs should have the correct signature once call sites are known; grKraid_801FF150(Ground_GObj*) remained empty but its prototype was corrected.
naming_conventions: Stage module functions retain address-based names with the grKraid_ prefix, e.g. grKraid_801FE440 and grKraid_801FEA00.
The recovered global config type is named after its symbol address as grKr_804D6A08_t, while its fields receive semantic names when evidence supports them.
Ground variable structs follow the existing grKraid_GroundVars and grKraid_GroundVars2 convention, with offset-preserving placeholder field names x0, x4, x8, xC, etc.
Config fields use descriptive snake_case names such as map_time_min, map_time_max, map_rot_spd_min, map_rot_spd_max, kraid_wait_time, kraid_wait_time_add, and kraid_pos_x.
Local decomp variables in the diff remain compiler/decompiler-style where semantics are uncertain, e.g. fVar1, fVar2, fVar3, iVar3, cVar3.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK(0x70) in grKraid_801FEA00 and PAD_STACK(0x10) in grKraid_801FEE54 to influence stack layout.
Added a static inline rand_inline helper around HSD_Randi to reproduce conditional random interval code paths for map timing.
Introduced a const zero Vec in sdata/rodata context and copied it into a local Vec before passing it to Camera_80030E44.
Preserved explicit floating-point comparisons against 0.0f and manual absolute-value patterns instead of replacing everything with library helpers.
Used direct frame-count comparisons such as xC == 80, 45, 55, 161, 246 and windows such as 147..169 or 123..138, reflecting the original animation/event timing.
Kept JObj flag operations and object-index lookups in-line with the state transitions, likely helping instruction ordering.
Typed the config struct and ground vars to recover data references and offsets rather than relying on casts from opaque memory.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that finish grKraid_801FE440, grKraid_801FE6D8, or grKraid_801FE818 from their remaining non-100% match percentages.
Search for uses of grKr_804D6A08_t to see whether field names or types were refined after this PR.
Look for subsequent cleanup of grKraid_GroundVars2 x0/x1/x2/x3/x4/x5 into semantic state-machine field names.
Compare other gr stage modules that use rand_inline or HSD_Randi range helpers for matching random wait timers.
Search for PRs involving Ground_801C2BA4 object indices in grkraid to verify object index meanings for map and companion GObjs.
